preferences: I want something that will genuinely scare me — not just jump scares, but the kind of horror that gets under your skin and stays with you for days. Something deeply unsettling with real dread and atmosphere.
I'm in the mood for a romantic comedy — something light and sweet with real chemistry between the leads. I want butterflies and laughs, not anything too heavy or dramatic.
Give me an epic sci-fi film with big ideas — space exploration, artificial intelligence, or time — something that makes me question reality and leaves me thinking about it for weeks.
I want to watch something based on a true story — a real event or real person that's so unbelievable it feels like fiction. I love when reality is more shocking than anything Hollywood could make up.
I don't know, just something really good to watch tonight. I'm open to anything as long as it's actually worth my time and not boring.
Something fun but also maybe a bit serious? I don't know — could be action or drama or comedy. I just want to feel something by the end of it.
I just got dumped and I want to feel something but I really don't want to cry. Something that hits emotionally but leaves me feeling okay or even good by the end.
I've had the most exhausting week of my life — work has been crushing me and I just want to decompress. I need something warm and comforting, like a cinematic hug.
My friends are coming over Friday night and we want something fun we can all enjoy together — lots of laughs, maybe some action, nothing too slow or too serious. We want to have a good time.
My younger siblings are visiting and I want to find something we can all watch together — something fun and adventurous that works for all ages, not just little kids but not too mature either.
I really love the kinds of movies I've been watching lately — intense, cerebral, visually stunning. Recommend me something in that same vein that I haven't seen yet.
I'm in the mood for something funny and entertaining, similar to the stuff I usually enjoy. Something that'll make me laugh out loud without being too dumb about it.
history: Bridesmaids, The Hangover Part II, La La Land
